title: .Docker.History .OCIv1.History and .History are the same thing
body:
Since tests are treating these as different fields, and they podman
inspect does not support the failover, we can not use these tests
against podman.

Removing .Docker.History and .OCIv1.History fixes the issue, and I
believe does not change anything.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@nalind Could you point out where my assumption is flawed.

~~I think this is overkill. Podman tests are failing only in podman-remote, not in podman. I think the solution to your podman PR is to get the `--omit-history` flag working in podman-remote.~~

EDIT: never mind. I see that you _did_ get the remote flag working. I should learn to not comment this late at night.

OK it's morning, my brain is fresh, but I'm still confused as to what problem this PR is intended to solve. Given your mention of `podman inspect`, my best guess is that this is a misunderstanding. Let me see if this helps: in the podman `buildah-bud` test suite, `run_buildah build` is replaced by `podman build` (or `podman-remote build`) but `run_buildah inspect` is left alone: it uses actual `buildah`, not podman. There is no place anywhere in buildah tests that use `podman inspect`. The tests which you update in this PR are, as best I can tell, all running and passing under podman. Confirmation: (1) I grepped for those test names in `apply-podman-deltas`, no hits; and (2) I grepped for those test names in [the log of your buildah-vendor PR](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6042439294124032/html/bud-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html), and every one of them is green, not yellow (yellow means skipped). Does this help, or am I still confused?

> @nalind Could you point out where my assumption is flawed.

I'm confused about why we're patching one of buildah's tests in an attempt to fix a problem that podman's having when it tries to reuse buildah's tests, when podman already applies patches to buildah's tests.

>fix a problem that podman's having when it tries to reuse buildah's tests

Can someone please tell me, very clearly and ELI5, what problem we're trying to solve? I still don't understand what problem we're seeing under podman. (Dan's PR is fully passing CI).
Thinking about this some more, if the PR is attempting to fix a problem being encountered elsewhere _by reducing test coverage here_, I can't see how I could approve of it.

Your right, I had too faulty assumptions, one is --omit-history was going to fail in podman -remote testing( it no longer does.)
And .Docker.History and .OCIv1.History did not exists in inspect, they must be hidden.
